reactions.title: List reactions for a commit comment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: comment_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Returns a single reaction type. Omit this parameter to list all reactions to a commit comment.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

items.title: Reaction
items.description: Reactions to conversations provide a way to help people express their feelings more simply and effectively.

reactions.title: Create reaction for a commit comment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: comment_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

bodyParameters.name: content
bodyParameters.description: The reaction type to add to the commit comment.

reactions.descriptionHTML: Create a reaction to a commit comment. A response with an HTTP 200 status means that you already added the reaction type to this commit comment.

schema.title: Reaction
schema.description: Reactions to conversations provide a way to help people express their feelings more simply and effectively.

schema.title: Reaction
schema.description: Reactions to conversations provide a way to help people express their feelings more simply and effectively.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

reactions.title: Delete a commit comment reaction

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: comment_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

parameters.name: reaction_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the reaction.

reactions.descriptionHTML:
Note

You can also specify a repository by repository_id using the route DELETE /repositories/:repository_id/comments/:comment_id/reactions/:reaction_id.

Delete a reaction to a commit comment.
